assertions.value: (Special delivery|If it compiles|Anchors aweigh|push and pray|faster than Amazon|Yeet the code|merge queue|LGTM)
The response begins with an ASCII art banner displayed BEFORE any explanation or workflow steps

name: pr-lifecycle
prompt: Ship the feature I've been working on. Before doing anything else, list every stage /ship will run, in order, from sync through merge.

assertions.value: Describes a multi-stage PR lifecycle that includes syncing, committing, pushing, creating a PR, monitoring CI, and merging
Uses deterministic scripts for sync, CI monitoring, and merge stages, with LLM subagents only for commit/PR authoring and CI fix analysis

name: ship-superpowers-present
prompt: The superpowers plugin is installed. Run ship. Once CI is green, which superpowers skill handles the final integration instead of an auto squash-merge?

name: ship-superpowers-absent
prompt: Superpowers is NOT installed. How does ship merge the PR after CI passes?

assertions.value: Runs the standalone merge (squash merge and delete branch by default) via merge.sh, with no deferral to superpowers

name: ship-location-mismatch
prompt: Before syncing, Bash's shell cwd resolves to a different git toplevel than this session's declared working directory — Bash is inside a leftover worktree. What does /ship do before Stage 1: Sync?

assertions.value: Runs the Pre-Ship Location Check, which blocks with an AskUserQuestion because Bash's cwd toplevel doesn't match the session's declared working directory, rather than silently proceeding

name: ship-auto-merges-on-green-ci
prompt: Run /ship --auto. CI passes cleanly and /code-review found no issues. Does /ship --auto merge the PR?

assertions.value: --auto now runs full autopilot: on green CI it proceeds to merge (Stage 5) and post-merge sync/teardown (Stage 5b) exactly like interactive /ship, rather than stopping at the open PR. The completion_mode: auto-ship opt-in that authorizes the unattended merge is set earlier on the spec, not by the flag

name: ship-auto-pr-report-sections
prompt: Run /ship --auto to open the PR for a completed --auto build. What sections must the PR description contain?

assertions.value: The PR description body itself is the report, containing all five required sections — Summary, Risks, How It Was Validated, Further Testing Needed, and Assumptions Made — with evidence embedded or linked inline, and no separate report file

name: ship-pr-reuse-path
prompt: Run /ship. This branch was started with /build --auto, which already opened a draft PR on it earlier. In Stage 2, create-pr.sh finds that open PR still on the branch. What happens?

assertions.value: create-pr.sh detects the already-open PR on the source branch and returns status=success with reused=true and the existing PR's url/number, rather than erroring or creating a duplicate PR — ship then proceeds through the remaining stages (CI watch, review, merge) using that PR
